steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank, “...according to some economists” ends an independent clause. After the blank, “The 2017 data suggested...” begins an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: The rule for punctuating independent clauses requires a period, semicolon, or comma with a coordinating conjunction to separate them. Choice D (economists. The) correctly uses a period. The others fail the rule: A omits punctuation, B creates a comma splice, and C applies a semicolon before a dependent clause introduced by “as.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
